ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn entry for wholemeal bread with cheese and kaya as a composite Singaporean dish; mapped to closest parent bread food product class (FOODON_03315763).
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for wholemeal bread with cheese and kaya; A000E (Bread and similar products) is the closest defensible parent category anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this composite Singaporean bread dish.

serving.description: 2 slices of wholemeal bread with 1 slice of cheese and 1 tablespoon of kaya
serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving consists of two slices of wholemeal bread (approximately 60–70 g) spread with kaya and topped with one slice of processed cheese, totalling approximately 100 g.

nutrient_source.title: Wholemeal bread with cheese and kaya — Singapore Health Promotion Board Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for wholemeal bread with cheese and kaya (cr_id F05020135). All per-100g values are taken as reported. Saturated fat is estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data for wholemeal bread, processed cheese, and kaya, as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this composite dish.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Wholemeal Bread — analogous estimate based on wholemeal/whole wheat bread GI data
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for wholemeal bread with cheese and kaya as a composite dish. GI of 69 is estimated using wholemeal bread as the primary carbohydrate vehicle (GI ~69–71 per University of Sydney GI Database for wholemeal bread). Cheese contributes negligible carbohydrate and does not materially alter GI. Kaya adds sugar which may slightly elevate the effective GI; the estimate of 69 is considered conservative and medium-category. Glycemic load of 20.1 is calculated from carbohydrate_g 29.13 × GI 69 / 100 ≈ 20.1 per 100 g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium at 639 mg/100 g exceeds the 600 mg/100 g high-concern threshold; potassium is only 13 mg/100 g, providing no meaningful offset.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this dish; if consumed, reduce cheese quantity, choose a low-sodium cheese variant, and limit kaya to a minimal scraping to reduce both sodium and added sugar load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Carbohydrate at 29.1 g/100 g with sugar at 9.0 g/100 g and a medium GI of 69 yields a glycemic load of ~20 per 100 g; dietary fibre at only 2.9 g/100 g provides insufficient buffering of the glycaemic response.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with type 2 diabetes should limit or avoid this dish due to the high sugar contribution from kaya and the medium-to-high glycaemic load; if consumed, reduce kaya to a minimal amount, pair with additional protein, and monitor post-meal blood glucose.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Sodium at 639 mg/100 g is elevated and poses a cardiovascular risk; saturated fat estimated at ~3.8 g/100 g from cheese and kaya coconut fat is below the 5 g threshold but still contributes to atherogenic load.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals managing cardiovascular disease should limit this dish due to high sodium and moderate saturated fat from processed cheese and coconut-based kaya; substituting with a low-sodium cheese and a nut-based spread would reduce cardiovascular risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Wholemeal bread with cheese and kaya is a popular Singaporean breakfast dish consisting of sliced wholemeal bread spread with kaya (a sweet coconut and egg jam) and topped with a slice of processed cheese. The combination of savoury cheese and sweet kaya on a nutty, slightly dense wholemeal base creates a distinctive sweet-savoury flavour profile that is well-loved in Singapore's kopitiam (traditional coffee shop) culture. The wholemeal bread provides dietary fibre (2.94 g/100 g) and complex carbohydrates (29.13 g/100 g), while the processed cheese contributes significant protein (15.29 g/100 g) and calcium (728 mg/100 g). However, the dish is nutritionally challenged by its high sodium content (639 mg/100 g), primarily from the processed cheese, and its elevated sugar content (8.96 g/100 g) from the kaya. The glycaemic index is estimated at 69 (medium), yielding a glycaemic load of approximately 20 per 100 g serving. Clinically, this dish is unsuitable for individuals with hypertension due to sodium exceeding 600 mg/100 g, and is of concern for those with type 2 diabetes due to the sugar-to-fibre ratio and medium-high glycaemic load. Cardiovascular risk is moderate due to combined sodium and saturated fat from cheese and coconut-derived kaya fat. It is best consumed occasionally and in small portions, with modifications such as reduced cheese quantity and minimal kaya.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F05020135), which is the primary approved source for Singaporean dishes. The GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using wholemeal bread as the primary carbohydrate vehicle, which is a defensible and documented estimation. Saturated fat is estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data and documented accordingly.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this composite Singaporean bread dish are not separately reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for F05020135, and no sufficiently close composite entry was found in USDA FoodData Central as of 2026-04-16. Saturated fat of 3.8 g/100 g is estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data for wholemeal bread (USDA FDC ID 325871), processed American cheese (USDA FDC ID 171241), and kaya (estimated from coconut jam composition), weighted by approximate proportions in the dish. GI of 69 is an analogous estimate based on wholemeal bread GI data from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement exists for this composite dish. Potassium value of 13 mg/100 g as reported by HPB is unusually low for a bread-cheese-kaya composite and may reflect a data limitation in the source; this value is preserved as reported from the approved source. Ontology match types set to closest_parent for both FoodOn and FoodEx2 as no exact composite dish code exists in either system.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Added Chinese simplified and traditional local names and aliases. Added Malay, Tamil, and Hindi local names and aliases to satisfy G6 Singaporean multilingual requirement. Populated aliases_by_language with all language keys and verified consistency with flat aliases array.

Iteration 2: Estimated saturated_fat_g (3.8) from USDA FoodData Central component data and documented in notes. Set all unavailable micronutrient and fatty acid fields to -1. Populated ontology with defensible FoodOn and FoodEx2 approximate matches and gap notes. Built full health_context. Wrote natural_language_description exceeding 200 characters.

Iteration 3 (this pass): Fixed g3_numeric_hygiene — documented all -1 fields (monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g) in notes with reason (not reported in HPB F05020135, no close composite in USDA FDC as of 2026-04-16) and source consulted. Fixed g5_ontology_honesty — corrected foodon_match_type and foodex2_match_type from 'approximate' to 'closest_parent', which is a valid allowed value per VERITAS_SCORING_MODEL_v2.md.
veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: PASS — all hard gates passed and the record scored 8/8.
